impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO advances the European Apply AI Strategy across all three priority sectors through validated demonstrators. The WP5 clinical decision support agent (OUT-4) — built on MAESTRO's neuro-symbolic planning and adaptive tri-store memory — demonstrates AI-assisted diagnostic reasoning on 500 de-identified clinical cases integrated with HL7 FHIR data sources, establishing a validated deployment pathway for high-risk AI in healthcare (D5-02). The WP6 manufacturing process optimisation agent (OUT-5) — validated in an operational pilot factory with digital twin integration over a three-month operational period — demonstrates real-time multi-agent coordination delivering measurable quality and energy efficiency improvements under ISIA leadership (D6-02). The WP7 logistics transfer demonstrator (OUT-6), led by ELI, additionally produces an architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving, completing documented coverage of all three Apply AI Strategy-identified application sectors (D7-02). Each demonstrator applies the MAESTRO architecture to a named sector and produces a structured evaluation report confirming AI agent capability deployment.
impact_pathways.tier2b_source_ref: expected_impacts.json:EI-01 | source_section: call_entry.scope | source_document: HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02.slice.json | status: Confirmed | matched_project_impacts: [IMP-2, IMP-3, IMP-4]

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO delivers EDIH-mediated industrial uptake across healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, and broader SME sectors. WP5 healthcare demonstrator results (OUT-4, D5-02) are disseminated to European hospital networks through at least two EDIHs under NIHS leadership, targeting engagement of ≥3 hospital networks in adoption planning by M48. WP6 manufacturing pilot evaluation (OUT-5, D6-02) is transferred to European manufacturing SMEs through EIT Manufacturing and at least two EDIHs under ISIA coordination. WP7 logistics outcomes (OUT-6, D7-02) are disseminated via ELI's EDIH connections across European logistics networks. WP9's open-source MAESTRO framework release (OUT-8, D9-02) and WP8 benchmark suite dataset (OUT-7, D8-02) provide shared infrastructure disseminated through at least three EDIHs, lowering barriers for European SMEs to deploy next-generation AI agent capabilities. All EDIH dissemination activities are coordinated by WP9 under BAL leadership.
impact_pathways.tier2b_source_ref: expected_impacts.json:EI-02 | source_section: call_entry.scope | source_document: HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02.slice.json | status: Confirmed | matched_project_impacts: [IMP-2, IMP-3, IMP-4, IMP-5]

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO advances open scientific infrastructure for the European AI research community via the AI-on-demand platform. The WP2 neuro-symbolic planning engine prototype (OUT-1, D2-02), WP3 adaptive memory system prototype (OUT-2, D3-02), WP4 coordination engine software release (OUT-3, D4-02), and WP8 unified MAESTRO integration platform incorporating the external tool orchestration layer (OUT-9, D8-01) are all contributed to the AI-on-demand platform as reusable open-science components under ATU, BIIS, CERIA, and FIIT leadership respectively. The MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset (OUT-7, D8-02), produced by FIIT-led WP8, is published as community evaluation infrastructure targeting adoption by ≥5 external research groups. The open-source MAESTRO framework (OUT-8, D9-02), released by BAL-led WP9, enables European researchers and SMEs to build on MAESTRO's scientific advances without platform dependency. All platform and open-source contributions are coordinated through WP9's AI-on-demand platform dissemination activities (T9-02).
impact_pathways.tier2b_source_ref: expected_impacts.json:EI-03 | source_section: call_entry.scope | source_document: HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02.slice.json | status: Confirmed | matched_project_impacts: [IMP-1, IMP-5]

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO strengthens European open strategic autonomy by establishing a European-origin neuro-symbolic AI agent paradigm that differentiates architecturally from US and Chinese approaches based on scale alone. The WP2 neuro-symbolic planning engine prototype (OUT-1, D2-02) and WP3 adaptive tri-store memory architecture prototype (OUT-2, D3-02), developed by ATU and BIIS respectively, constitute a European scientific approach to agent autonomy and robustness not dependent on proprietary non-European foundation model APIs. The WP4 formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol specification (OUT-3, D4-01), developed by CERIA, is submitted to standardisation bodies (ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE), establishing European-origin protocols as candidate international standards and reducing dependency on non-European agent interoperability frameworks. The WP4 coordination engine (D4-02) and WP8 MAESTRO integration architecture (OUT-9, D8-01) are released as open-source infrastructure through the WP9 MAESTRO framework release (OUT-8, D9-02), giving European researchers and SMEs full freedom to build AI agent solutions without dependency on proprietary non-European agent platforms. This pathway directly implements European open strategic autonomy in next-generation AI agent enabling technologies.

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO unlocks the economic potential of AI agents across diverse sectors by advancing foundational agent capabilities in planning, memory, coordination, and tool orchestration, then demonstrating those capabilities in operational sector deployments. The WP2 neuro-symbolic planning engine (OUT-1, D2-02) and WP3 adaptive memory architecture (OUT-2, D3-02) advance autonomous reasoning capabilities directly applicable to data analytics and coding domains identified in EI-05. The WP4 multi-agent coordination engine (OUT-3, D4-02) and WP8 external tool orchestration layer (OUT-9, D8-01) together enable AI agent deployment in complex multi-step economic workflows across these domains. Economic impact is demonstrated concretely in manufacturing (OUT-5, D6-02: ≥15% defect reduction, ≥10% energy savings in WP6 pilot factory; ≥5 SMEs engaged in technology transfer by M48) under ISIA leadership, and in logistics (OUT-6, D7-02: ≥20% disruption recovery improvement across ≥3 international supply chain corridors) under ELI leadership. The MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset (OUT-7, D8-02) and open-source framework (OUT-8, D9-02), produced by WP8 and WP9 respectively, enable ≥10 European SMEs and startups to build new AI agent products, amplifying economic impact beyond the project's direct sector demonstrators.

kpis.description: Hospital network adoption engagement from WP5 clinical decision support demonstrator, confirming MAESTRO's Apply AI Strategy healthcare sector impact pathway
kpis.target: ≥3 hospital networks engaged in post-project MAESTRO adoption planning by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Count of hospital network engagement agreements and letters of intent documented in the WP5 healthcare demonstrator evaluation report (NIHS, M42)

kpis.description: Manufacturing quality and energy performance improvements from WP6 pilot factory validation, confirming MAESTRO's Apply AI Strategy advanced manufacturing sector impact
kpis.target: ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings demonstrated in WP6 pilot factory over three-month operational period
kpis.measurement_method: Before/after operational KPI measurements from three-month pilot run, documented in WP6 manufacturing pilot evaluation report (ISIA, M42)

kpis.description: Architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving from WP7 cross-sector evaluation, completing Apply AI Strategy sector coverage including autonomous driving
kpis.target: Architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving completed and included in WP7 cross-sector evaluation report by M42
kpis.measurement_method: Completeness review of autonomous driving transferability mapping section in WP7 logistics cross-sector evaluation report (ELI, M42)

kpis.description: EDIH network active dissemination count for MAESTRO technology, confirming the EI-02 broader deployment mechanism through European Digital Innovation Hubs
kpis.target: ≥3 EDIHs actively disseminating MAESTRO technology by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Count of active EDIH partnerships with documented dissemination activities recorded in WP9 open-source framework release exploitation tracking (BAL, M36)

kpis.description: European SME and startup adoption of MAESTRO framework through EDIH-mediated channels, confirming industrial uptake pathway to broader economic benefit
kpis.target: ≥10 European SMEs/startups building AI agent applications on MAESTRO framework by M48
kpis.measurement_method: SME adoption count survey and framework repository analytics documented in WP8 benchmark suite dataset (FIIT, M48)

kpis.description: MAESTRO benchmark suite adoption by external research groups via AI-on-demand platform, confirming open scientific infrastructure contribution to European AI research community
kpis.target: MAESTRO benchmark suite adopted by ≥5 external research groups within project lifetime
kpis.measurement_method: Download and usage analytics from AI-on-demand platform, community engagement metrics, and third-party citation count documented in WP8 benchmark suite dataset (FIIT, M48)

kpis.description: Peer-reviewed publication output from MAESTRO research shared with European AI research community and indexed on AI-on-demand platform
kpis.target: ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in top AI venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, AAAI) by project end
kpis.measurement_method: Publication count tracked against scientific contribution milestones reviewed at WP8 integration architecture report checkpoint (FIIT, M24)

kpis.description: MAESTRO open-source framework community adoption rate, confirming European-origin AI agent ecosystem independence from non-European proprietary agent platforms
kpis.target: ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of D9-02 open-source framework release
kpis.measurement_method: GitHub repository star count at 6-month mark post-release of MAESTRO open-source framework (BAL, M36 release date + 6 months)

kpis.description: Standardisation proposal submission from WP4 formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol specification, confirming European contribution to international AI agent coordination standards
kpis.target: ≥1 standardisation proposal submitted to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE based on WP4 protocol specification by project end
kpis.measurement_method: Submission receipt confirmation and tracking reference number from standardisation body, documented against D4-01 protocol specification (CERIA, M18)

kpis.description: Citation impact of MAESTRO neuro-symbolic planning engine publications, confirming European AI research paradigm leadership and scientific influence
kpis.target: ≥100 citations of MAESTRO publications within 3 years of publication date
kpis.measurement_method: Google Scholar and Semantic Scholar citation tracking of publications derived from D2-02 neuro-symbolic planning engine prototype (ATU), measured at 3-year mark post-publication

kpis.description: Manufacturing SME technology transfer engagement from WP6 demonstrator, confirming unlocked economic potential of MAESTRO multi-agent manufacturing solutions
kpis.target: ≥5 manufacturing SMEs engaged in WP6-derived MAESTRO technology transfer by M48
kpis.measurement_method: SME engagement count documented in WP6 manufacturing pilot evaluation report and WP9 exploitation records (ISIA/BAL, M42 and M48)

kpis.description: Supply chain disruption recovery improvement from WP7 logistics demonstrator, confirming economic potential of MAESTRO agent architecture in logistics and cross-sector domains
kpis.target: ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time demonstrated operationally across ≥3 international supply chain corridors
kpis.measurement_method: Before/after disruption recovery time measurements from WP7 operational pilot across three corridors, documented in WP7 logistics cross-sector evaluation report (ELI, M42)
